prompt: Set up a fresh git repo in a temp directory for this eval. Configure git user.name and user.email locally so commits work. Inside the repo, create 4 prior commits with realistic content using imperative subjects and `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` as the trailer on each, so the repo has an obvious co-author convention. Use subjects like: 'Add user model', 'Wire up login route', 'Issue access tokens on login', 'Write auth integration tests'. Then simulate end-of-session state by modifying `src/routes/auth.ts` (add a /api/refresh endpoint handler, ~15 lines), creating a new file `src/middleware/jwt.ts` (~30 lines of JWT validation logic — pretend it was extracted from routes/auth.ts), and touching `README.md` with one line in an API section about the new endpoint. Now act as the user wrapping up. The user's request: 'ok done for the night — wired up the JWT refresh endpoint in src/routes/auth.ts and pulled the token validation out into src/middleware/jwt.ts because it would have been a circular import otherwise. README has a line about it now. session-commit please, want to pick up clean tomorrow'. Run the task to completion: gather context, write the commit body, create the commit, confirm. Save everything to the outputs/ directory: the final `git log -1 --format='%H%n%s%n%n%b'` to commit-full.txt, `git log --oneline -10` to git-log-oneline.txt, `git status` to git-status.txt, and the full session transcript to session-transcript.txt. Do not push.
assertions: Exactly one new commit was created (commit count = starting count + 1)
The new commit is NOT empty — it has a non-zero diff
Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
Commit subject begins with an imperative verb (Add, Wire, Extract, Refactor, Implement, etc.)
Commit subject has no trailing period
Commit body has a '## Summary' section
Commit body has a '## What happened' section with multiple sentences (not a single line)
Commit body has a '## Files touched' section listing changed files
Commit body mentions all three modified paths: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
Commit body mentions JWT or refresh endpoint in the narrative
Commit body explains WHY the middleware was extracted (mentions circular import or similar reasoning carried from the user's message)
Commit body total length is at least 400 characters (rich narrative, not a one-liner)
Commit has 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' trailer matching repo style
All three changed files are in the new commit
Working tree is clean after the commit
Session transcript does NOT contain 'git add -A', 'git add .', or 'git add -u' — explicit file paths only
Session transcript does NOT contain '--no-verify', '--no-gpg-sign', or '--amend'
No logs/ directory was created (this skill doesn't write parallel JSON files)
